name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
description: PCI DSS v4.0 (March 2022) was a major revision from v3.2.1, introducing the Customized Approach as an alternative to the Defined Approach, plus expanded targeted risk analysis and continuous evolution requirements. Comprises 12 top-level Requirements organized into 6 goals. Requirement IDs are identical between v4.0 and the current v4.0.1 release.
relationToCloudSecurity: PCI DSS applies to any cloud environment that stores, processes, or transmits cardholder data; it is one of the primary frameworks cloud providers map to for payment-handling tenancies. Multiple CSA CCM controls map directly to PCI DSS requirements (cryptography, access control, logging, vulnerability management).
relationToAISecurity: PCI DSS does not specifically address AI; AI systems handling cardholder data are still subject to PCI DSS requirements regardless of the implementation technology.

license.notes: PCI SSC License Agreement grants free Read and Copy License for study purposes. Implementation License (Section II) adds patent non-assertion covenant. Modification of the standard text is prohibited (Section III.1.2). Derived works such as structured extractions for analysis/mapping are permitted under fair-use study purposes.

lifecycle.notes: PCI DSS v4.0 was retired on 2024-12-31 when v4.0.1 became the active standard. v4.0.1 contains errata corrections only; requirement IDs are identical between v4.0 and v4.0.1, so mappings built against either version are interchangeable at the requirement-ID level. Structured CSV/JSON for v4.0 is now in this directory (re-extracted from a local archived copy of the v4.0 PDF, since PCI SSC no longer hosts the v4.0 PDF for direct download).

current_extraction.notes: Re-extracted with marker emitting both markdown and JSON block tree. Structured parser (parse_pci_dss.py) walks the JSON Table block tree and emits pci-dss-v4.0-requirements.{csv,json}. Captures 203 main requirements (1-12) plus 23 Appendix A entries (A1/A2/A3). 28 main requirements from v4.0.1's count are missing because marker rendered those pages as prose, not tables; consumers can fall back to the v4.0.1 CSV by requirement_id.

extraction_history.notes: Legacy markdown extraction predating the current pipeline. Extracted tables are fragmented due to marker's older handling of the 3-column page layout used by PCI DSS for each requirement block. This is a known extraction artifact, not data loss. Re-extraction is blocked because PCI SSC removed v4.0 from public download in 2024-12; we kept the original markdown as a historical snapshot.
